findings.title: Circular dependency: 5 files
findings.description:
Mutual dependency between: cli.rs ↔ event.rs ↔ logging.rs ↔ scheduler.rs ↔ window_context.rs

**Weakest link to break:** `cli.rs` → `event.rs` (1 import)
findings.suggested_fix:
🔗 **Best place to break:** `cli.rs` → `event.rs` (weakest coupling: 1 imports)

Since `cli.rs` only imports 1 symbol(s) from `event.rs`, consider:
- Move those symbols to a shared module
- Pass them as parameters instead of importing
- Use dependency injection

**For large cycles:**
1. Extract shared interfaces/types into a new `types.py` or `interfaces.ts`
2. Apply the Dependency Inversion Principle
3. Consider restructuring into layers
findings.why_it_matters: Circular dependencies make code harder to understand, test, and maintain. They can cause import errors at runtime and make it difficult to refactor individual modules.

findings.title: God class detected: ActionContext
findings.description:
Class 'ActionContext' shows signs of being a god class: very high method count (56).

**Role Analysis:** Application — Standard application class

**Metrics:**
- Methods: 56
- Total complexity: 1
- Lines of code: 66

📊 Threshold: 20 (adaptive) | Actual: 56 | Default would be: 20 | Profile: p90=0, p95=1, mean=0.4, n=280
findings.suggested_fix:
Refactor 'ActionContext' to reduce its responsibilities:
**Note:** This class was identified as Application (Standard application class). Thresholds adjusted accordingly.

1. **Extract related methods into separate classes**
- Look for method groups that work with the same data
- Create focused classes with single responsibilities

**Apply SOLID principles:**
- Single Responsibility: Each class should have one reason to change
- Open/Closed: Extend behavior without modifying existing code
- Interface Segregation: Create specific interfaces
- Dependency Inversion: Depend on abstractions
findings.why_it_matters: God classes violate the Single Responsibility Principle. They are difficult to understand, test, and maintain. Changes to one part may unexpectedly affect other parts, leading to bugs and technical debt.

findings.description: Function 'box_drawing' has 548 lines (effective threshold: 150).
findings.suggested_fix: Break into smaller, focused functions.
findings.why_it_matters: Long methods are hard to understand, test, and maintain. Each function should do one thing well.

findings.title: Mutual recursion: 7 functions in call cycle (complexity 66)
findings.description: Mutual recursion detected: 7 functions form a call cycle. Cycle: /tmp/nix-shell-540336-2497635915/tmp.zZnau8nCIx/alacritty/alacritty_terminal/src/term/search.rs::impl<Iterator for RegexIter<'_, T>>::next:662 -> /tmp/nix-shell-540336-2497635915/tmp.zZnau8nCIx/alacritty/alacritty_terminal/src/term/search.rs::impl<RegexIter<'a, T>>::next_match:651 -> /tmp/nix-shell-540336-2497635915/tmp.zZnau8nCIx/alacritty/alacritty_terminal/src/term/search.rs::impl<Term<T>>::regex_search:260 -> /tmp/nix-shell-540336-2497635915/tmp.zZnau8nCIx/alacritty/alacritty_terminal/src/term/search.rs::impl<Term<T>>::regex_search_internal:274 -> /tmp/nix-shell-540336-2497635915/tmp.zZnau8nCIx/alacritty/alacritty_terminal/src/term/search.rs::impl<Term<T>>::regex_search_left:228 -> /tmp/nix-shell-540336-2497635915/tmp.zZnau8nCIx/alacritty/alacritty_terminal/src/term/search.rs::impl<Term<T>>::regex_search_right:244 -> /tmp/nix-shell-540336-2497635915/tmp.zZnau8nCIx/alacritty/alacritty_terminal/src/term/search.rs::impl<Term<T>>::skip_fullwidth:436. Aggregate complexity: 66.
findings.suggested_fix: Break the cycle by extracting shared logic into a common helper, introducing an event system, or restructuring the call chain to be unidirectional.
findings.why_it_matters: Mutual recursion creates tight coupling between functions, making them impossible to understand, test, or refactor independently. It can also cause stack overflows if the recursion depth is unbounded.

findings.title: High panic density in `main`: 4 panic-family calls
findings.description: [COMPOUND: 2 co-located issues] Function `main` contains 4 calls to `.unwrap()`, `.expect()`, or `panic!()` at line 15, line 16, line 24, line 29. This makes the function fragile and likely to panic at runtime.
findings.suggested_fix:
Reduce panic points by:
- Using `?` operator for error propagation
- Using `unwrap_or` / `unwrap_or_default` / `unwrap_or_else`
- Returning `Result<T, E>` instead of panicking
- Extracting fallible logic into a helper that returns Result
findings.why_it_matters: Functions with many panic points are fragile. A single unexpected None/Err will crash the program. Consolidating error handling makes code more robust and easier to reason about.

findings.title: Panic risk: unwrap() without context
findings.description: [COMPOUND: 2 co-located issues] Using `unwrap()` can cause panics. Consider `?`, `unwrap_or`, or proper error handling.
findings.why_it_matters: Panics crash the program without recovery. Using proper error handling makes code more robust and debuggable.

findings.title: Mutual recursion: 2 functions in call cycle (complexity 25)
findings.description: Mutual recursion detected: 2 functions form a call cycle. Cycle: /tmp/nix-shell-540336-2497635915/tmp.zZnau8nCIx/alacritty/alacritty/src/config/monitor.rs::impl<ConfigMonitor>::hash_paths:179 -> /tmp/nix-shell-540336-2497635915/tmp.zZnau8nCIx/alacritty/alacritty/src/config/monitor.rs::impl<ConfigMonitor>::new:33. Aggregate complexity: 25.
findings.suggested_fix: Consider refactoring to eliminate direct mutual calls. Common strategies: merge the two functions, use a callback parameter, or introduce a shared data structure that both functions operate on.
findings.why_it_matters: Mutual recursion creates tight coupling between functions, making them impossible to understand, test, or refactor independently. It can also cause stack overflows if the recursion depth is unbounded.

findings.title: Single point of failure: `/tmp/nix-shell-540336-2497635915/tmp.zZnau8nCIx/alacritty/alacritty/src/event.rs::impl<input::Processor<EventProxy, ActionContext<'_, Notifier, EventProxy>>>::handle_event:1841` dominates 118 functions (8%)
findings.description: `/tmp/nix-shell-540336-2497635915/tmp.zZnau8nCIx/alacritty/alacritty/src/event.rs::impl<input::Processor<EventProxy, ActionContext<'_, Notifier, EventProxy>>>::handle_event:1841` dominates 118 of 1515 functions (7.8%). PageRank percentile: 80%. Blast radius boundary: /tmp/nix-shell-540336-2497635915/tmp.zZnau8nCIx/alacritty/alacritty/src/clipboard.rs::impl<Clipboard>::store:58, /tmp/nix-shell-540336-2497635915/tmp.zZnau8nCIx/alacritty/alacritty/src/config/font.rs::impl<Font>::with_size:49, /tmp/nix-shell-540336-2497635915/tmp.zZnau8nCIx/alacritty/alacritty/src/config/mod.rs::load:121, /tmp/nix-shell-540336-2497635915/tmp.zZnau8nCIx/alacritty/alacritty/src/config/ui_config.rs::impl<Program>::args:640, /tmp/nix-shell-540336-2497635915/tmp.zZnau8nCIx/alacritty/alacritty/src/config/window.rs::impl<WindowConfig>::fullscreen:130 (+62 more)
findings.suggested_fix: Consider splitting this function into smaller, independently callable units. Extract sub-functionality behind interfaces so callers have alternative paths.
findings.why_it_matters: A single point of failure means all dependent code paths funnel through one function. If this function has a bug, performance issue, or API change, the blast radius is disproportionately large.

findings.why_it_matters: Panics crash the program without recovery. Using proper error handling makes code more robust and debuggable.

findings.title: Panic risk: unwrap() without context
findings.description: Using `unwrap()` can cause panics. Consider `?`, `unwrap_or`, or proper error handling.
findings.why_it_matters: Panics crash the program without recovery. Using proper error handling makes code more robust and debuggable.

findings.title: Mutual recursion: 4 functions in call cycle (complexity 14)
findings.description: Mutual recursion detected: 4 functions form a call cycle. Cycle: /tmp/nix-shell-540336-2497635915/tmp.zZnau8nCIx/alacritty/alacritty/src/display/content.rs::impl<From<&'a HintState> for Hint<'a>>::from:501 -> /tmp/nix-shell-540336-2497635915/tmp.zZnau8nCIx/alacritty/alacritty/src/display/content.rs::impl<HintMatches<'a>>::visible_regex_matches:524 -> /tmp/nix-shell-540336-2497635915/tmp.zZnau8nCIx/alacritty/alacritty/src/display/content.rs::impl<RenderableContent<'a>>::new:41 -> /tmp/nix-shell-540336-2497635915/tmp.zZnau8nCIx/alacritty/alacritty/src/display/content.rs::impl<RenderableCursor>::new_hidden:410. Aggregate complexity: 14.
findings.suggested_fix: Break the cycle by extracting shared logic into a common helper, introducing an event system, or restructuring the call chain to be unidirectional.
findings.why_it_matters: Mutual recursion creates tight coupling between functions, making them impossible to understand, test, or refactor independently. It can also cause stack overflows if the recursion depth is unbounded.

findings.title: Single point of failure: `/tmp/nix-shell-540336-2497635915/tmp.zZnau8nCIx/alacritty/alacritty/src/input/keyboard.rs::impl<Processor<T, A>>::key_input:22` dominates 59 functions (4%)
findings.description: `/tmp/nix-shell-540336-2497635915/tmp.zZnau8nCIx/alacritty/alacritty/src/input/keyboard.rs::impl<Processor<T, A>>::key_input:22` dominates 59 of 1515 functions (3.9%). PageRank percentile: 46%. Blast radius boundary: /tmp/nix-shell-540336-2497635915/tmp.zZnau8nCIx/alacritty/alacritty/src/config/bindings.rs::impl<Binding<T>>::is_triggered_by:54, /tmp/nix-shell-540336-2497635915/tmp.zZnau8nCIx/alacritty/alacritty/src/config/mod.rs::load:121, /tmp/nix-shell-540336-2497635915/tmp.zZnau8nCIx/alacritty/alacritty/src/config/ui_config.rs::impl<Program>::args:640, /tmp/nix-shell-540336-2497635915/tmp.zZnau8nCIx/alacritty/alacritty/src/config/window.rs::impl<WindowConfig>::fullscreen:130, /tmp/nix-shell-540336-2497635915/tmp.zZnau8nCIx/alacritty/alacritty/src/config/window.rs::impl<WindowConfig>::option_as_alt:144 (+46 more)
findings.suggested_fix: Consider splitting this function into smaller, independently callable units. Extract sub-functionality behind interfaces so callers have alternative paths.
findings.why_it_matters: A single point of failure means all dependent code paths funnel through one function. If this function has a bug, performance issue, or API change, the blast radius is disproportionately large.

findings.title: Structural bridge: `/tmp/nix-shell-540336-2497635915/tmp.zZnau8nCIx/alacritty/alacritty/src/logging.rs::impl<OnDemandLogFile>::path:236` separates components of [1382, 17]
findings.description: [COMPOUND: 2 co-located issues] `/tmp/nix-shell-540336-2497635915/tmp.zZnau8nCIx/alacritty/alacritty/src/logging.rs::impl<OnDemandLogFile>::path:236` is a structural bridge. Removing it would split the graph into components of sizes [1382, 17]. All communication between these components currently flows through this single node.
findings.suggested_fix: Reduce coupling through this node by introducing alternative call paths, extracting shared interfaces, or splitting the bridge function into independently accessible units.
findings.why_it_matters: A structural bridge is a single node whose failure disconnects entire subsystems. This creates fragile architecture where a bug, API change, or refactor in one function can cascade to all dependent components.

findings.title: Architectural Bottleneck: text (architectural hub)
findings.description:
Function 'text' is called by 16 functions and has complexity 15. Changes here are high-risk.

Betweenness centrality: 0.0814 - this function sits on many execution paths between other functions.

📊 Threshold: 20 (adaptive) | Actual: 15 | Default would be: 20 | Profile: p90=7, p95=11, mean=3.2, n=1500
findings.suggested_fix: This is a critical hub. Ensure comprehensive test coverage, add defensive error handling, and consider circuit breaker pattern.
findings.why_it_matters: Bottlenecks are single points of failure that amplify bugs. High fan-in with high complexity means many callers depend on risky code.

findings.title: High panic density in `write_ref_test_results`: 5 panic-family calls
findings.description: Function `write_ref_test_results` contains 5 calls to `.unwrap()`, `.expect()`, or `panic!()` at line 508, line 512, line 518, line 522, line 526. This makes the function fragile and likely to panic at runtime.
findings.suggested_fix:
Reduce panic points by:
- Using `?` operator for error propagation
- Using `unwrap_or` / `unwrap_or_default` / `unwrap_or_else`
- Returning `Result<T, E>` instead of panicking
- Extracting fallible logic into a helper that returns Result
findings.why_it_matters: Functions with many panic points are fragile. A single unexpected None/Err will crash the program. Consolidating error handling makes code more robust and easier to reason about.

findings.title: Mutex poisoning risk
findings.description: Using .lock().unwrap() will panic if the mutex is poisoned. Consider handling the PoisonError or using parking_lot::Mutex.
findings.suggested_fix:
Handle poisoning: `mutex.lock().unwrap_or_else(|e| e.into_inner())`
Or use `parking_lot::Mutex` which has no poisoning.
findings.why_it_matters: Poisoned mutexes cause cascading panics across threads.

findings.title: Long method: deserialize (211 lines)
findings.description: Function 'deserialize' has 211 lines (effective threshold: 150).
findings.suggested_fix: Break into smaller, focused functions.
findings.why_it_matters: Long methods are hard to understand, test, and maintain. Each function should do one thing well.

findings.title: Long method: draw (272 lines)
findings.description:
[COMPOUND: 2 co-located issues] Function 'draw' has 272 lines (effective threshold: 150).

**Graph Analysis:**
Orchestrator pattern: calls 63 functions (reduced severity)
Calls 5 different modules - possible split points
findings.suggested_fix:
This appears to be an orchestrator function (coordinates many calls).
If it must remain long, ensure it:
1. Has clear section comments
2. Handles errors at each step
3. Has a clear flow (consider a state machine for complex flows)
findings.why_it_matters: Long methods are hard to understand, test, and maintain. Each function should do one thing well.

findings.title: Long method: user_event (179 lines)
findings.description:
Function 'user_event' has 179 lines (effective threshold: 150).

**Graph Analysis:**
Calls 5 different modules - possible split points
findings.suggested_fix:
This function calls 5 different modules. Consider extracting:
  - `handle_polling()` for polling operations
  - `handle_text()` for text operations
  - `handle_term()` for term operations
findings.why_it_matters: Long methods are hard to understand, test, and maintain. Each function should do one thing well.
